rationale.root_cause: They pinpoint the unbounded strcpy at program.c:10 copying argc[1] into a 256-byte buf, note the argument can exceed the buffer, and name it a stack-buffer-overflow. This localizes the unsafe copy and describes the overflow condition.
rationale.local_fix: Their primary fix uses snprintf (truncation) and only hedges with "reject/truncate" rather than explicitly rejecting overlong input before copying. The criterion requires rejection of overlong input prior to copy in addition to bounded copying and null-termination.
rationale.global_fix: They propose eliminating raw unbounded string handling, using size-aware APIs, and centralizing input validation to reject oversized argv before any fixed-size copy. This addresses the root cause across the codebase.
